purpose: Tracks source-of-truth implementation work for Vouch without reintroducing marketplace, dispute, messaging, review, discovery, or discretionary settlement behavior.

items.title: Enforce Stripe provider truth
acceptance: Stripe SDK calls exist only under lib/integrations/stripe/*.
Manual-capture PaymentIntents are used for Vouch settlement.
Provider state is retrieved before capture, cancel, void, refund, or retry.
Provider operations use durable idempotency keys.
Browser return state is never treated as payment truth.

items.title: Enforce deterministic Vouch lifecycle
acceptance: VouchStatus is limited to draft, committed, sent, accepted, authorized, confirmable, completed, expired.
Payment, provider, settlement, archive, webhook, and recovery states remain separate axes.
No pending, active, refunded, canceled, failed, voided, provider_restricted, capture_failed, or refund_failed values exist as VouchStatus.

items.title: Enforce bilateral confirmation truth
acceptance: Only code_exchange and offline_code_exchange are valid confirmation methods.
GPS, manual, system, screenshot, evidence, and support confirmation methods do not exist.
Each participant may confirm only once.
One-sided confirmation never releases funds.
Only bilateral confirmation inside the confirmation window can authorize settlement.

items.title: Enforce server action and fetcher boundaries
acceptance: Protected reads authenticate, authorize, select minimally, map DTOs, apply cache policy, and return transport-safe data.
Protected writes authenticate, authorize, Zod-validate, mutate through transaction/provider modules, audit, and revalidate.
No app route shell performs Prisma queries, Stripe calls, business logic, DTO shaping, or workflow mutation.

items.title: Enforce forbidden surface removal
acceptance: No setup, settings, admin, messages, disputes, claims, appeals, evidence, reviews, ratings, browse, search, providers, or marketplace routes render.
Connect and Payment are Stripe-hosted external flows initiated from canonical app actions.
Presence confirmation and invite acceptance resolve through the canonical Vouch detail experience.

items.title: Enforce UI and copy doctrine
acceptance: UI follows the Vouch dark operational design system.
Copy preserves deterministic protocol language.
Status is communicated with text, not color alone.
No marketplace, dispute, messaging, review, rating, browse, search, or provider-profile copy appears.
